pr.title: Use shasum command on macOS, sha1sum otherwise
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Updated the root Makefile so the SHA1 checksum command is selected by host OS: macOS/Darwin uses `shasum`, while other platforms use `sha1sum`. This moved SHA1SUM setup out of the Windows/non-Windows split, making checksum verification work on macOS without breaking Linux or Windows-style builds.
what_changed: Removed the Windows-specific `SHA1SUM := sha1sum` assignment from the `ifeq ($(WINDOWS),1)` branch.
Closed the Windows/non-Windows block before assigning `SHA1SUM`, so checksum command selection is independent of the CPP/WINE/devkitPPC path setup.
Added an OS check using `ifeq ($(shell uname),Darwin)`.
Set `SHA1SUM := shasum` on Darwin/macOS and `SHA1SUM := sha1sum` otherwise.
smart_moves: Separated host checksum tool detection from the existing Windows versus non-Windows compiler/toolchain setup, reducing duplicated or misplaced platform assumptions.
Used `uname` to target Darwin specifically, matching the platform where `shasum` is typically available by default.
Kept the command variable name `SHA1SUM` stable, minimizing the change surface for downstream Makefile rules that already reference it.
Preserved `sha1sum` as the default fallback for non-Darwin systems.
decomp_lessons: Build reproducibility depends on small host-tool differences; checksum helpers should be selected based on host OS rather than assumed globally.
When adding macOS support to Makefiles, prefer a narrow Darwin check instead of changing defaults for all non-Windows users.
Keep platform conditionals scoped to the variable they actually control; unrelated assignments inside a Windows/non-Windows block can hide portability bugs.
For decompilation projects with strict artifact verification, checksum command portability is part of the matching workflow even when no decompiled source changes.
naming_conventions: The Makefile uses uppercase variables for tool commands, e.g. `SHA1SUM`, `CPP`, `AS`, `CC`, `WINE`, `DEVKITPPC`.
The checksum variable remains named `SHA1SUM` even when backed by the macOS `shasum` command, preserving semantic intent rather than mirroring the exact executable name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function matching tactics were present in this PR.
The relevant matching-support tactic is build-environment normalization: choose the correct checksum executable so verification steps can run consistently across host platforms.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.text: @CelestialAmber lmk if this still works for you
review_feedback.interpretation: The author requested confirmation from a macOS-affected user or reviewer that the portability fix still worked.

review_feedback.interpretation: A user confirmed the Makefile change worked in their environment.

follow_up_queries: Find other Makefile host-tool assumptions that differ between macOS and Linux.
Search for uses of `SHA1SUM` to confirm all checksum rules work with both `shasum` and `sha1sum` output formats.
Check later PRs for additional Darwin or macOS build-system fixes.
Audit whether `$(shell uname)` is used consistently elsewhere in the build system.
